message:
fix: harden database operations with read-only access, staged seed imports, and safer error recovery

Indexer: when baseline historical seed import fails, attempt to reopen the
canonical database before quarantining — only quarantine and recreate if the
reopen also fails. This preserves existing canonical data when the seed source
is the problem (e.g. schema too old) rather than the canonical DB itself.

CLI: introduce open_franken_cli_read_db() which opens databases with
SQLITE_OPEN_READ_ONLY and PRAGMA query_only=1, preventing accidental writes
from analytics and status probe paths. Simplify probe_state_db() by removing
the thread+channel timeout indirection — the read-only connection with
busy_timeout achieves the same timeout semantics without spawning a thread
per probe.

Storage: replace the destructive delete-then-vacuum-into seed pattern with a
staged approach — VACUUM INTO a temporary file on the same filesystem,
validate it, then atomically rename to the canonical path. This eliminates the
window where a crash would leave the canonical DB deleted but not yet replaced.
Remove the redundant remove_database_files() call on seed loop failure since
the staged pattern never touches the canonical path until validation passes.

Add 3 new tests: read-only analytics DB enforcement, meta probe without count
scan, and failed baseline seed preserving existing canonical data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
